rulings.Case Title: CAVALRY SPV I, LLC, AS ASSIGNEE OF CITIBANK, N.A. VS. ELIANA V BELL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Thursday, Jun-25-2026. LINE 12. DEFENDANT ELIANA CHANG, SUED AS ELIANA V. BELL AKA ELIANA BELL'S, MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Continued for hearing to July 21, 2026 on the court's own motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: KEEP AMERICA SAFE AND BEAUTIFUL VS. FLORAFLEX CORP. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Thursday, Jun-25-2026. LINE 2. PLAINTIFF KEEP AMERICA SAFE AND BEAUTIFUL'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment. The court grants the unopposed motion to approve the Proposition 65 settlement and consent judgment. The court finds that the warning required by the settlement is in compliance with Proposition 65 (Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5 to 25249.14); that the award of attorney's fees pursuant to the settlement is reasonable under California law; and that, considering the factors set out in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7, subdivision (b)(2), and the facts asserted in the moving papers, the penalty amount is reasonable. Accordingly, the motion is granted and the parties may submit the proposed consent judgment for signature. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) rulings.Case Title: DIANA CLOOS VS. KIMBERLY GREEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Thursday, Jun-25-2026. LINE 11. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE AMENDED COMPLAINT. The motion for leave to file a first amended complaint is granted. Leave to amend is liberally granted and should be denied only where there is undue delay, prejudice, or futility. The court does not find any of those circumstances. There is sufficient time before trial for defendants to conduct discovery and make dispositive motions. Plaintiff provides adequate explanation for the delay in the pleading. With respect to futility, defendants provide no evidence on this motion that they have stipulated to vicarious liability. With respect to alter ego, the proposed pleading is sufficiently definite and defendants' disagreement with the factual allegations in the pleading is not a basis to deny amendment. Plaintiff shall file the amended pleading within one week. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Case Title: NOURA TULIMAL VS. ZIHUI TONG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Thursday, Jun-25-2026. LINE 6. 2-DEFENDANT ZIHUI TONG'S Motion To Compel Arbitration (Joinder). Defendant Zihui Tong's joinder the in arbitration motion brought by defendants Uber Technologies et al is denied. Tong does not show that he is a party to the arbitration agreement or that plaintiff Noura Tulimal has agreed to arbitrate claims against him, nor does he show any basis for the court to enforce the arbitration agreement as to a nonparty to the agreement. rulings.Case Title: ALLEGRA COHEN VS. CHARLES SCHWAB & CO., INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Thursday, Jun-25-2026. LINE 7. PLAINTIFF ALLEGRA COHEN'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. The unopposed motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted. The court treats it as an unusual summary judgment motion in which the plaintiff presents evidence that she is entitled to judgment and the defendant does not make procedural or substantive objections to the entry of judgment. The parties have presented evidence that trustee Barbara Simon was acting as Sheba Sweet's agent in signing the November 26, 2020 beneficiary designation. The November 26, 2020 beneficiary designation is therefore an enforceable agreement. The court will sign the order attached as Exhibit A to the Cohen Declaration. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: JAMES MCCARTY ET AL VS. CLAUDIO MARTONFFY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Thursday, Jun-25-2026. LINE 8. 1-DEFENDANTS CLAUDIO MARTONFFY AN INDIVIDUAL, and CLAUDIO MARTONFFY DESIGN, INC'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendants Claudio Martonffy and Claudio Martonffy Design, Inc.'s (collectively Martonffy) motion to strike punitive damages allegations from the complaint is denied. Punitive damages are available upon proof by clear and convincing evidence of malice, oppression, or fraud. Fraud means "an intentional misrepresentation, deceit, or concealment of a material fact known to the defendant with the intention on the part of the defendant of thereby depriving a person of property or legal rights or otherwise causing injury." (Civ. Code, Sec. 3294, subd. (c)(3).) Plaintiffs have alleged intentional misrepresentation, i.e. fraud, by Martonffy in their fifth cause of action. This pleading is sufficient to sustain a prayer for punitive damages. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.
